purpose: Second-pass research atlas that turns current frontier research into product invention deltas beyond W598. It focuses on low-bit native models, FP4/NVFP4 recovery, runtime policy fusion, RAG compilers, PEFT/adapters, LLM security, autoscaling, and evidence systems.

sources.lesson: 1.58-bit ternary models are a distinct model family rather than a post-training quantization knob; Kolm should detect when a native low-bit student target is better than compressing a dense BF16 teacher.
bitnet.cpp shows that native ternary inference can be an edge runtime strategy; Kolm should model ternary runtimes as first-class artifact targets with CPU energy and memory receipts.
An open native 1-bit 2B-scale report means small CPU-first students can be a real product target; Kolm should test native-low-bit students for cheap local classifiers, extractors, and agents.
TensorRT-LLM supports a matrix of NVFP4, MXFP4, FP8, INT8, AWQ, GPTQ, and KV-cache formats; Kolm needs target-aware numeric-format selection rather than generic quantization labels.
Quantization-aware distillation for NVFP4 recovers accuracy through KL-style student training; Kolm should combine distillation strategy and quantization oracle when a target supports FP4.
TorchAO is becoming the PyTorch quantization center for inference workflows; Kolm should treat torchao recipes as package/release-sensitive adapters, not hard-coded backend assumptions.
TorchAO float8 training claims meaningful throughput gains at scale; Kolm should expose training precision as a strategy-brain decision with reproducibility receipts.
Torch-TensorRT uses ModelOpt QDQ flows for INT8, FP8, and FP4; Kolm compile receipts should record the inserted QDQ graph and builder precision flags.
vLLM speculative decoding has testable distribution-preservation expectations; Kolm runtime policy should verify draft/target settings rather than blindly enabling speculation.
Hidden-state extraction can support speculator training and diagnostics; Kolm should capture safe intermediate evidence for draft-model and quality-debug workflows.

Ray Serve LLM combines OpenAI compatibility, multi-LoRA, autoscaling, monitoring, prefill/decode patterns, and engine abstraction; Kolm cloud deploy plans should emit equivalent serving topology contracts.
Autoscaling depends on request concurrency and replica policy; Kolm should translate artifact SLOs into serving autoscaler settings and receipts.
Managed spot jobs can recover from preemption and lower GPU cost; Kolm's cloud broker should preserve checkpoint and artifact lineage across interrupted jobs.
Kueue uses quota-aware admission for Kubernetes jobs; Kolm enterprise queues should model admitted, waiting, preempted, and resumed training states.
AWS Batch GPU jobs require accelerator-aware job definitions; Kolm BYOC plans should emit exact job specs instead of generic cloud instructions.
R2 presigned URLs enable direct temporary dataset/artifact transfer; Kolm jobs should use short-lived signed object paths and receipt expiry metadata.
GraphRAG combines extraction, graph/network analysis, prompting, and summarization; Kolm should compile RAG artifacts that include graph build receipts and source lineage.

Recursive abstractive tree retrieval improves long-document reasoning by retrieving at multiple abstraction levels; Kolm should compile hierarchical retrievers when data needs global and local context.
Late-interaction retrieval gives stronger matching than plain dense embeddings for some tasks; Kolm RAG strategy should benchmark retriever choice, not assume one embedding backend.
MIPROv2 jointly optimizes instructions and examples; Kolm should try prompt-program optimization before distillation when the task can be solved by better instructions and demonstrations.
PEFT supports LoRA, QLoRA-style all-linear training, DoRA caveats, initialization choices, and layer replication; Kolm should choose adapter strategy from data and deployment constraints.
Predibase sets the bar for adapter deployment and Turbo LoRA serving; Kolm must add proof, offline artifact portability, and compiler/runtime receipts to beat that adapter-first UX.
OpenPipe validates prompt-to-fine-tune capture loops; Kolm should add capture filtering, K-score intervals, and artifact promotion instead of just fine-tuning from traces.
Immutable experiment snapshots are the right primitive for eval comparison; Kolm should attach immutable eval snapshots to every artifact promotion.
LangSmith ties evaluation, observability, prompt engineering, and deployment; Kolm should make these concerns artifact-native and receipt-backed.
LLM judge outputs need interval evaluation and uncertainty; Kolm should expose K-score confidence, abstention, and uncertainty propagation in promotion gates.
Doubly robust and calibrated judge estimation addresses off-policy evaluation bias; Kolm replay evaluation should include overlap and calibration diagnostics.
OWASP LLM risks define the minimum threat model for prompt injection, insecure outputs, poisoning, denial of service, sensitive disclosure, and agent/tool misuse.
Garak probes LLM systems for vulnerabilities; Kolm should integrate red-team probes into compile/promotion receipts for agent and public runtime surfaces.
GenAI semantic conventions cover model, agent, metrics, events, and provider attributes; Kolm receipts should map to these fields without default content capture.
Sigstore makes signing and verification easier for open ecosystems; Kolm release and artifact verification should follow similar usability expectations.
SLSA defines provenance expectations; Kolm packages and artifacts need builder identity, source hashes, dependencies, and reproducibility status.
MCP standardizes agent-tool connections; Kolm should treat compiled MCP tools as signed artifacts with conformance, budgets, and policy receipts.

invention_deltas.title: Native Ternary Student Target
invention_deltas.product_change: Add a build-strategy branch that can recommend a native low-bit student instead of post-training quantizing a dense model when the target is CPU, edge, browser, or cheap local inference.
build_steps: Add `native_low_bit_student` as a candidate build action in the strategy brain.
Model ternary runtimes separately from INT4/FP4 post-training quantization.
Require holdout K-score comparison against prompt-only, dense student, and quantized dense student baselines.
Record CPU memory, energy class, latency, model family, and runtime verifier in the receipt.
Reject this path for tasks needing unsupported modalities, long context, or unavailable runtime packages.
failure_modes: Treating ternary as a generic quant method instead of a native model family.
Promoting low-bit student without holdout parity.
Claiming mobile/browser availability before packages exist.

invention_deltas.title: FP4 Distillation Recovery Loop
invention_deltas.product_change: Fuse the quantization oracle with distillation strategy so FP4/NVFP4 targets can run a recovery loop instead of accepting raw post-training quality loss.
build_steps: Detect target runtime support for FP4/NVFP4/MXFP4 and available calibration rows.
Create a `quantization_aware_distill` recommendation when raw FP4 quality confidence crosses the risk band.
Use KL-style teacher/student recovery when the teacher provider is policy-allowed.
Receipt the numeric format, QDQ graph or runtime format, recovery dataset hash, and quality interval.
Fail closed when FP4 is unsupported, teacher policy blocks recovery, or public benchmark evidence is missing.
failure_modes: FP4 selected because it is fastest even when quality floor fails.
QDQ or builder flags are not captured in receipts.
Local synthetic recovery is marketed as public benchmark data.

invention_deltas.title: Runtime Policy Fusion Planner
invention_deltas.product_change: Make runtime policy a compiled artifact field covering speculative decoding, prefix reuse, hidden-state diagnostics, quantized KV cache, streaming, and telemetry.
build_steps: Represent speculative decoding, prefix caching, KV quantization, and streaming as runtime policy fields.
Attach runtime-specific constraints for vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, ONNX Runtime, IREE, WASM, and local runtimes.
Use hidden-state diagnostics only when policy and runtime permit it.
Emit rejected runtime policies with reasons.
Record safe OTEL attributes without content capture by default.
failure_modes: Speculative decoding changes output distribution without verification.
Prefix cache crosses tenant/privacy boundaries.
Runtime telemetry stores prompts by default.

invention_deltas.title: SLO Serving Autoscaler
invention_deltas.product_change: Translate artifact SLOs into serving topology: replicas, max ongoing requests, prefill/decode split, fallback target, queue policy, and scale-to-zero limits.
build_steps: Accept SLO inputs: p50/p95/p99, max cost per 1k calls, warm/cold policy, and privacy mode.
Generate Ray/Kubernetes/BYOC serving topology with replica and queue policy.
Attach fallback provider/runtime policies and cost ceilings.
Emit webhooks for scale, overload, fallback, and SLO breach.
Receipt observed SLO metrics and autoscaler config hash.
failure_modes: Autoscaler lowers cost by violating latency SLO.
Fallback provider violates compliance policy.
Serving topology cannot be reproduced from receipt.

invention_deltas.title: RAG Artifact Compiler
invention_deltas.product_change: Compile retrieval pipelines into .kolm artifacts with retriever choice, index build hash, graph/tree/chunk lineage, source policy, citation verifier, and K-score evals.
build_steps: Add retriever candidates: dense, sparse, hybrid, ColBERT late interaction, RAPTOR tree, GraphRAG graph, and no-RAG.
Score candidates by task type, document structure, update frequency, privacy, latency, and citation strictness.
Compile index manifest and source lineage into the artifact.
Add citation verifier and retrieval K-score evals.
Receipt index build hash, retriever version, source permissions, and failed citation checks.
failure_modes: RAG answer passes judge but cites wrong source.
Index updates are not reflected in artifact version diff.
Private source documents leak through shared retrieval cache.

invention_deltas.title: Prompt Program Optimizer Before Training
invention_deltas.product_change: Insert a prompt/program optimization branch before training so Kolm tries cheaper instruction/demo search when the task can improve without model weight changes.
build_steps: Detect tasks where examples and instructions dominate model behavior.
Run prompt/demo candidate generation against holdout evals before training.
Use K-score confidence intervals to decide if optimization is enough.
Compile optimized prompt program as a signed artifact when it meets the floor.
Recommend distillation only when prompt optimization plateaus.
failure_modes: Training starts before cheaper prompt optimization is tested.
Prompt candidates overfit the holdout.
Optimized prompt artifact lacks versioned evidence.

invention_deltas.title: Adapter Hypervisor
invention_deltas.product_change: Add a strategy layer that chooses LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, full fine-tune, prompt optimization, RAG, or distillation based on data size, target hardware, adapter overhead, merge policy, and proof needs.
build_steps: Add adapter strategy candidates to distill strategy output.
Score adapter choice by trainable parameter budget, inference overhead, mergeability, target runtime, and data volume.
Block adapter training when the deployment target cannot serve adapters safely.
Receipt adapter config, base model hash, merge state, and eval interval.
Expose the adapter strategy as a build plan alternative, not a standalone product branch.
failure_modes: LoRA selected for a target that cannot serve adapters.
Adapter merge state is missing from artifact receipt.
Adapter improves training eval but regresses runtime latency.

invention_deltas.title: Security Red-Team Compiler
invention_deltas.product_change: Make red-team probes part of compile and agent-tool promotion, with OWASP class coverage, garak-style probe manifests, tool-policy checks, and receipt-backed failures.
build_steps: Map OWASP LLM risks to artifact and MCP tool policies.
Add probe manifests for prompt injection, data leakage, tool misuse, denial of service, and insecure output handling.
Run local red-team fixture probes before promotion.
Receipt probe versions, pass/fail rows, mitigations, and accepted residual risk.
Block public/agent deployment when critical probes fail.
failure_modes: Agent tool can call outside declared policy.
Prompt injection probe failures are treated as warnings.
Security page claims certification based only on local probes.

invention_deltas.title: OTEL Receipt Bridge
invention_deltas.product_change: Map Kolm compile/runtime/eval/provider/agent receipts to safe OpenTelemetry GenAI spans and immutable experiment snapshots.
build_steps: Define receipt-to-OTEL attribute mapping for model calls, agent spans, tool calls, evals, compile jobs, and artifact verification.
Keep content capture opt-in and policy-gated.
Emit immutable experiment IDs for K-score and benchmark snapshots.
Expose export settings for Datadog, Honeycomb, Grafana, Splunk, and local files.
Verify no secrets or raw PHI/PII leak into span attributes.
failure_modes: OTEL attributes contain prompt content by default.
Eval snapshot can be edited after promotion.
Trace IDs are not linked to artifact receipts.

invention_deltas.title: Signed Release Rail
invention_deltas.product_change: Turn package release blockers into a signed release rail with checksums, provenance, clean-install logs, and artifact storage records for every SDK/runtime/install channel.
build_steps: Create one release manifest per package target with version, registry URL, checksum, provenance, and clean-install log.
Sign release artifacts and attach SLSA-style provenance.
Store release bundles in configured artifact storage or GitHub release.
Update readiness only after the manifest validates with no placeholders.
Generate docs/install copy from the release manifest.
failure_modes: Install docs advertise packages before signed releases exist.
Homebrew/winget checksums remain placeholders.
Package docs drift from manifest names.

invention_deltas.title: Cloud Scheduler Arbitrage
invention_deltas.product_change: Upgrade the cloud broker into a scheduler that can choose local, SSH, RunPod, AWS Batch, Kubernetes/Kueue, spot/preemptible, or provider execution based on deadline, budget, privacy, and checkpointability.
build_steps: Accept deadline, budget ceiling, data residency, checkpoint interval, and preemption tolerance.
Score compute targets by availability, cost, policy, artifact transfer, and recovery support.
Attach object storage and checkpoint path for every non-local job.
Emit provider-specific job specs and queue/admission states.
Receipt preemptions, retries, cost deltas, and final artifact hashes.
failure_modes: Spot/preemptible job loses progress because checkpointing is missing.
Cheapest compute violates data residency.
Cloud job has no artifact output path.

invention_deltas.title: Public Benchmark Reproducer
invention_deltas.product_change: Separate local fixture evidence from public benchmark evidence with signed raw outputs, hardware/provider manifests, model versions, pricing cards, and confidence intervals.
build_steps: Define public benchmark manifests with dataset version, model/provider version, hardware, runtime, and price assumptions.
Store raw outputs, receipts, and scoring logs as signed bundles.
Report K-score intervals, abstention, replay overlap, and judge calibration.
Block public leaderboard claims until raw bundles are published.
Generate benchmark pages from verified evidence state.
failure_modes: Synthetic tests are shown as public benchmark data.
Competitor/provider versions are unpinned.
Leaderboard lacks raw reproducible output.

invention_deltas.title: Standard Conformance Kit
invention_deltas.product_change: Make .kolm adoption easier by shipping verifier, conformance fixtures, runtime packets, MCP tool schema checks, OTEL mapping, and provenance examples as a public kit.
build_steps: Package spec grammar, verifier examples, conformance fixtures, MCP tool schema tests, and OTEL mapping examples.
Publish local validation commands that run without hosted Kolm access.
Create upstream adoption packets for runtimes and registries.
Keep foundation/adoption status as external gates.
Record external PRs, releases, and partner evidence in readiness workorders.
failure_modes: Conformance kit requires hosted API credentials.
External adoption is claimed before merge/release evidence.
MCP tool conformance omits budget and tool-policy checks.

invention_deltas.title: Active Data Engine
invention_deltas.product_change: Turn captured traffic into prioritized labeling and evaluation queues: uncertain rows, high-cost rows, low-overlap rows, redaction failures, failed citations, and security probe failures.
build_steps: Compute row priority from uncertainty, cost impact, privacy status, redaction risk, overlap, and failure class.
Generate label queues and eval queues with tenant/RBAC controls.
Feed prioritized rows into prompt optimization, RAG repair, distillation, or no-train diagnostics.
Receipt why each row was selected or excluded.
Respect zero-retention by using metadata-only signals when content capture is disabled.
failure_modes: Sensitive content enters label queue under zero-retention.
Low-value rows dominate labeling budget.
Uncertainty signals are not tied to promotion gates.
